title_en: Your First Humanoid Robot Coworker Will Probably Be Chinese
title_zh: 你的第一位类人机器人同事很可能是中国制造的

paragraphs.en: The 4-foot-tall humanoid robot that’s in front of me seems, quite honestly, a bit drunk. It leaps from one leg to the other, waving its arms. After 30 seconds or so it abruptly stops, then strides toward me with an arm outstretched.
paragraphs.zh: 我面前这台4英尺高的人形机器人，说实话，看起来有点像喝醉了。它一会儿在一条腿上跳、一会儿换另一条腿，手臂还在挥舞。大约30秒后，它会突然停下，然后伸出一只手向我迈步走来。

paragraphs.en: The little robot is at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference, on the banks of the Huangpu river in Shanghai. The convention center is teeming with humanoids—dancing ones, box-toting ones, robot dog-walking ones doing circuits around trade show booths. A few lie slumped in a corner as their batteries recharge.
paragraphs.zh: 这台小机器人在上海黄浦江畔的世界人工智能大会现场。我看到展馆里挤满了各种人形机器人：跳舞的、拿箱子的、牵着机器狗在展位周围来回巡逻的。也有一些机器人躺在角落里，低头歪倒着，等待电池充电。

paragraphs.en: The robot uses a front-facing depth camera and a 3d lidar scanner that provides a view of both the front and back.
paragraphs.zh: 这台机器人搭载了一台前置深度摄像头和一台三维激光雷达扫描仪，可同时提供前后视角。

paragraphs.en: It’s a balmy day in July, and I’ve come to Shanghai to learn how China’s AI world differs from the Western one I usually cover. I’d immediately noticed the sleek electric cars from BYD, Xiaomi, and Huawei that fill the streets of the city’s fashionable old French Concession. At Manner, a high-end coffee chain, I sampled an avant-garde beverage called a “sparkling citrus iced Americano.” Now that I’m at the convention, I can’t help but picture these dancing robots strolling Shanghai’s streets and perhaps carrying their owner’s shopping bags.
paragraphs.zh: 7月的上海天气很温暖。我来到这里，就是为了了解中国的人工智能世界与我平时报道的西方圈子有何不同。刚到时，我就注意到流行的法国租界里，满街都是来自比亚迪、小米和华为的时尚电动车。后来在Manner这一高端连锁咖啡店，我试了一杯名为“气泡柑橘冰美式咖啡”的前卫饮品。来到会议现场后，我不禁想象这些会跳舞的机器人正在上海街头闲庭信步，也许还帮主人提着购物袋。

paragraphs.en: A group crowds around a small boxing ring, where two humanoids throw punches at each other. One falls down, then gets back on its haunches and straightens up. A young woman offers to let me punch a humanoid. I feel certain that this would make me feel bad, so I decline.
paragraphs.zh: 一群人围在一个小型拳击台前，两台类人机器人互相出拳。一个机器人倒下后，又撑着后腿重新站起并挺直身体。一位年轻女子提出让我去打一下其中一台机器人。我很确定这会让我难受，所以我婉拒了。

paragraphs.en: The activity in this convention center is a bit of a mirage, of course. Amid the throngs, you can spot people holding game controllers. They’re giving the robots high-level instructions. The humanoids can control their balance and execute short routines, but it’s humans who tell them which way to walk, whom to shake hands with, or when to backflip. Another limitation: Many humanoids don’t have fingers. Their arms usually end in stumps, meaning that while they can hold and lift boxes (or wear boxing gloves, they can’t grasp anything.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，这个展会现场的活动有点像“海市蜃楼”。在人群里，你还能看到一些人拿着游戏手柄，他们正在给机器人下达高层指令。这些类人机器人可以自己控制平衡并执行短时动作流程，但真正告诉它们该往哪走、跟谁握手、或者何时后空翻的还是人类。另一个限制是：许多类人机器人没有手指。它们的手臂通常以残端结束，这意味着它们虽然能拿起和搬动箱子（或者戴上拳击手套），却拿不住任何东西。

paragraphs.en: At the International Robot Exhibition in Tokyo, Unitree G1 robots fight during a kick-boxing demonstration.
paragraphs.zh: 在东京国际机器人展上，优锻（Unitree）G1机器人在踢拳演示中进行搏斗。

paragraphs.en: Here are 23 ways China is rewiring the future.
paragraphs.zh: 以下是中国正在重塑未来的23种方式。

paragraphs.en: Even so, experts predict that robots will have profound effects on the workforce and the economy. Amazon is testing humanoids from an American startup called Agility and, according to leaked memos, expects to replace a significant number of workers with robots in the next few years. Bank of America analysts predict that by 2035, robot makers will ship 10 million humanoids a year. Morgan Stanley forecasts that by 2050 a billion will be in use, with almost a third of them—302.3 million—in China, compared with 77.7 million in the US.
paragraphs.zh: 即便如此，专家仍预计机器人将对劳动力市场和经济产生深远影响。亚马逊正在测试一家名为 Agility 的美国初创公司的类人形机器人，并且根据外泄备忘录显示，它预计在未来几年内会用机器人取代大量员工。美国银行分析师预测，到 2035 年，机器人制造商每年将出货 1000 万台类人形机器人。摩根士丹利则预测，到 2050 年将有 10 亿台在使用，其中近三分之一，也就是 3.023 亿台，位于中国；而美国为 7,770 万台。

paragraphs.en: Perhaps no humanoid maker has a bigger lead than a Hangzhou-based company called Unitree. While Elon Musk’s Optimus staggers through its demos, Unitree’s robots are doing sprints, kung-fu kicks, and acrobatic backflips. (The conference’s dancing door greeter was a Unitree.) Unitree’s legged robots are also incredibly cheap, costing tens of thousands of dollars or less, a tenth of what a typical humanoid in the US costs. Unitree is China’s most prominent robotics startup, a national champion for its tech industry, and is reportedly targeting a $7 billion IPO listing in Shanghai. And if Unitree fails? A staggering 200-plus other Chinese companies are also developing humanoids, which recently prompted the Chinese government to warn of overcapacity and unnecessary replication. The US has about 16 prominent firms building humanoids.
paragraphs.zh: 或许没有哪家类人形机器人制造商的领先优势比杭州的零界（Unitree）更明显。尽管埃隆·马斯克的 Optimus 在演示中举步维艰，零界的机器人却在做冲刺、踢功夫腿法和杂技式的后空翻。（会议现场跳舞迎宾机器人就是零界的。）零界的足式机器人也非常便宜，价格在几万美元以内，约为美国普通类人形机器人的十分之一。零界是中国最知名的机器人初创公司，也是中国科技行业的一家“国家冠军”，据称正将于上海推进高达 70 亿美元规模的 IPO 上市。若零界失败怎么办？还有多达 200 多家中国公司也在开发类人形机器人，这一趋势近期已促使中国政府警告存在产能过剩和重复建设的问题。美国大约有 16 家主要公司正在打造类人形机器人。

paragraphs.en: With stats like that, one can’t help but suspect that the first country to have a million humanoids will be China.
paragraphs.zh: 看了这些数字，不禁会猜测，率先拥有一百万台类人形机器人的第一个国家，可能会是中国。

paragraphs.en: Unitree’s home city of Hangzhou is about 110 miles from Shanghai. The place is famous for its vast and photogenic West Lake, but it’s also home to Alibaba, the ecommerce giant, Ant Group (one of Alibaba’s affiliates), and several fast-growing AI startups. Besides Unitree, this includes DeepSeek, whose open source AI models enjoy global popularity.
paragraphs.zh: 零界的家乡杭州距离上海约 110 英里。这个地方以其广阔且非常上镜的西湖闻名，但它同时也是阿里巴巴电商巨头、其关联公司蚂蚁集团，以及多家快速成长的人工智能初创公司的所在地。除了零界之外，这里还包括 DeepSeek，它的开源人工智能模型在全球都很受欢迎。

paragraphs.en: I’m hoping to visit Unitree along with a few other companies. Unlike many of its rivals, Unitree sells a wide range of legged robots and is profitable. Unfortunately, I find the company largely off-limits to visitors as it prepares for the IPO. I later hear that the headquarters is battleworn: A set of stairs inside the entrance is battered from legged robots practicing carrying heavy payloads. “It is one of the most run-down places you can imagine,” says Mike Cho, a robotics entrepreneur based in Singapore who has spent time there. “Everywhere there are broken robots, broken parts, and broken concrete.”
paragraphs.zh: 我希望能和其他几家公司一起去 Unitree 参观。与很多竞争对手不同，Unitree 经营着种类很广的腿式机器人，并且已经盈利。不幸的是，我发现这家公司在准备上市期间基本对外封闭，几乎不接待访客。我后来得知，公司的总部看起来像“被打磨过的战场”：入口处的一段楼梯因为两足机器人反复练习搬运重物而被蹂躏得严重受损。新加坡的一位机器人创业者 Mike Cho 说，他去过那儿后评价道，“这是你能想象到的最破旧的地方之一。到处都是坏掉的机器人、损坏的零件和破碎的水泥。”

paragraphs.en: Later I connect with Wang Xingxing, Unitree’s CEO, who, citing his poor English, agreed to talk via the messaging app WeChat. Wang is something of a celebrity in China. In February 2025, he was invited to an event hosted by the Chinese premier, Xi Jinping, along with several members of China’s tech royalty (Alibaba’s Jack Ma; Huawei’s founder and CEO, Ren Zhengfei; BYD’s chairman and CEO, Wang Chuanfu; and the CEO of Xiaomi, Lei Jun). At the conference in Shanghai, I’d seen Wang deliver a keynote speech in rapid-fire Mandarin. He told the crowd that he planned to build robots adept at “serving tea, working in factories, or even performing arts.”
paragraphs.zh: 之后我通过微信联系到了 Unitree 的首席执行官王兴兴。在说明自己英语不太好的情况下，他同意在微信上交流。王在中国算得上名人。2025 年 2 月，他受邀参加中国总理习近平主办的活动，与中国科技界几位“王者”同台：马云、任正非、王传福以及雷军。那天在上海的会议上，我曾看到王以很快的节奏做中文主题演讲。他向现场表示，自己计划研发能够“端茶服务、在工厂工作，甚至参与演艺”的机器人。

paragraphs.en: On WeChat, he tells me that he’s been fascinated by robots since he was a kid. During his first year at university, he cobbled together a small, two-legged robot on a budget of 200 RMB ($28). “The moment I saw the robot I designed come to life,” Wang writes, “the sense of achievement from combining technology and creativity made me determined to devote myself to robotics research.”
paragraphs.zh: 在微信上，他告诉我，自己从小就对机器人着迷。大学第一年，他用 200 元人民币（28 美元）的预算拼装出了一台小型两足机器人。“当我看到自己设计的机器人第一次活动起来的那一刻，”王写道，“技术与创造力结合带来的成就感让我下定决心，把人生献给机器人研究。”

paragraphs.en: As a master’s student at Shanghai University in 2015, he built a small, four-legged robot for less than 20,000 RMB (about $2,800). It became a social media hit; people marveled that a student could build such a sure-footed, low-cost machine. He worked briefly for the drone company DJI before receiving a small amount of seed funding and establishing Unitree in 2016. The company released its first product, a robot dog called Laikago, in 2017.
paragraphs.zh: 2015 年，王在上海大学攻读硕士期间，用不到 20,000 元人民币（约合 2,800 美元）打造出一台小型四足机器人。它一经发布便在社交媒体上走红，人们惊叹一名学生竟能做出如此稳健、成本如此低廉的机器。随后他在大疆无人机公司短暂任职，后来拿到一笔小额种子融资，于 2016 年创办了 Unitree。公司于 2017 年发布了第一款产品——一款名为 Laikago 的机器狗。

paragraphs.en: Wang admits that, early on, Boston Dynamics and its fourlegged robot Spot were an inspiration. Gavin Kenneally, CEO of Ghost Robotics, recalls seeing Laikago at trade shows and conferences. “Their robot really wasn’t very capable,” he tells me. But while Spot sold for $75,000, Laikago cost only $25,000.
paragraphs.zh: 王承认，最初波士顿动力公司和它的四足机器人 Spot 曾给他带来灵感。Ghost Robotics 的首席执行官 Gavin Kenneally 回忆说，他在展会和会议上看到了 Laikago。“他们的机器人其实并不太强大。”他告诉我。不过，Spot 的售价是 75,000 美元，而 Laikago 只要 25,000 美元。

paragraphs.en: In 2019, a team at MIT developed an electric motor that would allow its quadruped, Mini Cheetah, to run at record speeds. Less than a year later, Unitree unveiled a new fourlegged robot, A-1. “It was basically an exact copy of the MIT Mini Cheetah,” Kenneally says. “It was leaps and bounds ahead of their previous platform.”
paragraphs.zh: 2019 年，麻省理工学院的一个团队开发出一种电机，使它的四足机器人 Mini Cheetah 能以创纪录的速度奔跑。不出一年，Unitree 推出了一款新型四足机器人 A-1。Kenneally 说：“它几乎是 MIT Mini Cheetah 的精确复制品。”他接着补充：“它比他们之前的平台要领先了一个档次。”

paragraphs.en: Unitree’s business took off. In 2023 its sales of quadrupeds were 10 times higher than Boston Dynamics’, according to the consulting firm SemiAnalysis. Nearly 24,000 Unitree quadrupeds were sold for use, among other places, on construction sites and oil rigs and in factories. The robots can climb stairs and traverse rubble to conduct inspections or security patrols.
paragraphs.zh: Unitree 的业务起飞了。咨询公司 SemiAnalysis 的数据显示，2023 年，Unitree 的四足机器人销售额是波士顿动力的 10 倍。Unitree 共售出了近 24,000 台四足机器人，应用场景包括建筑工地、石油钻井平台和工厂等。机器人可以上下楼梯、越过碎石和废墟，用于巡检或安保巡逻。

paragraphs.en: Disassemble a Unitree robot and you’ll see the incredible advantages of China’s tightly integrated manufacturing, where strong relationships with suppliers can make or break a company. SemiAnalysis looked at one configuration of Unitree’s newest quadrupeds, Go2, priced at about $8,000. All of its core components—the sensors, motors, gearbox, battery, computer—cost only $3,272, the consulting firm found. (Unitree prices other Go2 configurations as low as $1,600.) “What they’ve done is to iterate extremely quickly, using Chinese manufacturing and supply chains,” Kenneally tells me.
paragraphs.zh: 拆解一台 Unitree 机器人，你会看到中国高度一体化制造体系的巨大优势，在这里，与供应商的紧密关系足以成就或打垮一家企业。SemiAnalysis 研究了 Unitree 最新四足机器人 Go2 的一种配置，售价约为 8,000 美元。咨询公司发现，它的所有核心部件——传感器、电机、齿轮箱、电池和计算机——总成本仅为 3,272 美元。（Unitree 其他 Go2 配置的起售价低至 1,600 美元。）Kenneally 对我说：“他们所做的就是利用中国制造和供应链能力，实现了极快的迭代。”

paragraphs.en: Wang wasn’t only interested in robot dogs. Boston Dynamics had been working on a humanoid called Atlas for years. By 2023 Wang was watching Musk whip up enthusiasm for humanoids with his Optimus demos, and Wang started to develop a humanoid of his own. In August of that year, Unitree released its first model, H1. Less than a year after that, Unitree started selling the more capable G1, which costs a little over $13,500 for academic users. This past summer, the company unveiled an even cheaper but less advanced humanoid called R1, which sells for just 39,999 RMB ($5,700).
paragraphs.zh: 王不仅对机器人狗感兴趣。波士顿动力已经研究了多年一款名为 Atlas 的类人机器人。到2023年，王看到埃隆·马斯克借助 Optimus 的演示大幅炒热了“类人机器人”话题，于是他也开始开发自己的类人机器人。同年8月，优必选发布了第一款型号 H1。不到一年后，优必选开始销售性能更强的 G1，面向学术用户的售价略高于13,500美元。这个夏天，公司又推出了更便宜但技术能力较弱的 R1，售价仅39,999元人民币（约5,700美元）。

paragraphs.en: During his WAIC speech, Wang had hinted that a revolution in robotic intelligence is on the horizon. “The ‘ChatGPT moment’ for robots will come when a robot can enter an unfamiliar room, take a bottle of water to someone, or tidy up autonomously after a simple command,” he told attendees. “This could happen in one to three years if fast, or three to five years at most.”
paragraphs.zh: 在WAIC演讲中，王曾暗示机器人智能革命就在眼前。他对与会者说：“机器人的‘ChatGPT时刻’将到来，当一个机器人能走进陌生的房间、把一瓶水送给某人，或在一句简单命令后自行整理环境时，这一时刻就会出现。”他补充道：“如果进展很快，这可能在一到三年内发生，最多也许三到五年。”

paragraphs.en: Plenty of roboticists believe they’ll get their “ChatGPT moment” soon enough, though the question, of course, is how that’s defined. I had reason to suspect, though, that Wang leans optimistic. At a recent major robotics conference in Hangzhou, a panel of experts surveyed the room on whether humanoids would take over most human jobs by 2050. Wang was one of the few who thought they would.
paragraphs.zh: 不少机器人学专家认为他们很快就会迎来自己的“ChatGPT时刻”，尽管问题当然在于如何界定这一时刻。我之所以这样怀疑，是因为我有理由认为王的态度偏乐观。在最近于杭州举办的一场大型机器人大会上，一组专家在台上调查了与会者：他们是否认为到2050年类人机器人会接管大多数人类工作。王是为数不多认为会发生的少数人之一。

paragraphs.en: I hurry to catch a flight to Beijing to visit another outfit eyeing robots’ ChatGPT moment. The Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence is a nonprofit founded in 2018 by the city government to coordinate AI research. From a window in the BAAI’s robotics lab, on an upper floor of a squat office building, I can see the sprawling campuses of Tsinghua University and Peking University, two of China’s most prestigious schools. Despite its academic outlook, the BAAI has attracted the US government’s attention. In May 2025, the US Commerce Department added the institute to its Entity List, barring it from acquiring American technology. The US government accuses the BAAI of “attempting to acquire U.S.-origin items in support of China’s military modernization,” although staff at the BAAI insist it is simply a research lab.
paragraphs.zh: 我匆忙赶去北京赶飞机，拜访另一个正在盯紧机器人“ChatGPT时刻”的团队。北京人工智能研究所（BAAI）是2018年由北京市政府成立的非营利机构，目标是统筹人工智能研究。站在这家位于一栋矮平办公楼高层的 BAAI 机器人实验室窗前，我可以看到清华大学和北京大学那两所中国最负盛名高校绵延的校园。尽管它以学术研究为导向，BAAI 仍引起了美国政府的注意。2025年5月，美国商务部将该机构列入“实体清单”，禁止其获得美国技术。美国政府指控 BAAI“试图获取美国来源物项，以支持中国的军队现代化”，而 BAAI 的工作人员则坚持认为它只是一个研究实验室。

paragraphs.en: At the academy I meet another dancing Unitree robot. This one assumes a wide stance, then stomps its legs and pumps its fists to a beat. I hadn’t seen these moves before, and for good reason. The humanoid is using an experimental approach, in which an AI model learns by observing human movement—in this case, dance routines from Douyin, China’s version of TikTok.
paragraphs.zh: 在学院里，我又遇到一台会跳舞的 Unitree 机器人。它先是摆出一个很宽的站姿，然后踩着节拍用腿重重跺地并用力挥动拳头。我以前没见过这种动作，原因很充分：这台人形机器人采用了一种实验性的做法，让一个人工智能模型通过观察人的动作来学习——在这个案例里，是学习抖音（TikTok 的中国版）上的舞蹈套路。

paragraphs.en: Unitree G1 robots show off their dance moves during a store opening at a mall in Beijing.
paragraphs.zh: 北京一处商场开业活动中，Unitree G1 机器人展示了它们的舞蹈动作。

paragraphs.en: Zhongyuan Wang, president of the BAAI and head of its robotics work, tells me about the institute’s new open-source robotics model, Robobrain 2.0, which its engineers built to merge a language model’s abilities with spatial reasoning. Robots have yet to take full advantage of language models’ broad capabilities, and the hope is that LLMs will help robots operate in unfamiliar situations and communicate better. In one experiment, BAAI researchers used Robobrain 2.0 to control a pair of robot arms. When the model heard someone say “I’m hungry, make me a burger,” it translated the statement into actions that commanded the robot arms to place lettuce and meat in a bun.
paragraphs.zh: 北京智源人工智能研究院（BAAI）院长、中国科学院高工等、也是其机器人负责人王仲元告诉我，他们新的开源机器人模型 Robobrain 2.0。它是由工程师构建的，目的是把语言模型的能力与空间推理结合起来。机器人迄今还没有充分利用语言模型那种广泛的能力，而希望通过大语言模型（LLM）帮助机器人在陌生情境下更好地行动，并改善沟通能力。在一项实验中，BAAI 研究人员用 Robobrain 2.0 控制一对机械臂。当模型听到“我饿了，给我做一个汉堡”时，它会把这句话转化为动作指令，驱动机械臂把生菜和肉放进面包里。

paragraphs.en: But language models can only do so much, and what robots need most urgently is a new kind of model that understands the physical world the way an LLM understands the written word. And to build that model, engineers need way more data. While walking around BAAI, I see scores of workers behind desks. They’re teleoperating various robot arms and grippers to teach algorithms simple manipulation tasks such as sweeping up beans on a table, pouring liquids from a jug into different cups, and picking items from shelves. A young man wearing a virtual reality headset appears to be making tea as a camera records his every move. The idea is that with enough training data, robots will intuit how to do all sorts of things without specific training.
paragraphs.zh: 但语言模型能做的事情终究有限，机器人最迫切需要的是一种新模型——它能像 LLM 理解书面语言那样理解物理世界。要构建这种模型，工程师们需要远比现在更多的数据。在 BAAI 参观时，我看见许多工作人员坐在桌边。他们通过遥控操作各种机械臂和夹爪，教算法学习一些简单的操作任务，比如把豆子从桌面扫拢、把液体从壶倒入不同的杯子、从货架上取物。一个戴着虚拟现实头显的年轻人似乎正在冲泡茶水，摄像头则记录着他的一举一动。其想法是，只要有足够的训练数据，机器人就能凭经验在不经过专项训练的情况下，学会做各种事情。

paragraphs.en: The trouble is, nobody quite knows what data is most useful to the robots, let alone how much they need or how best to collect it. And for humanoids to become ubiquitous, people need to invent hardware that better mimics a human hand. For a robot, doing a backflip is a lot easier than flipping a coin.
paragraphs.zh: 问题在于，没人真正清楚对机器人来说什么数据才最有用，更别说它们到底需要多少数据以及如何最佳地采集。并且，要让仿人机器人普及，人们还需要发明更像人手的硬件。对机器人来说，做一个后空翻要比掷硬币容易得多。

paragraphs.en: Major joints have permanent magnet synchronous motors that provide high torque and rapid acceleration, allowing it to perform explosive moves.
paragraphs.zh: 主要关节采用永磁同步电机，能够提供高扭矩和快速加速，使它能够完成爆发式动作。

paragraphs.en: Still, Tony Zhao, cofounder and CEO of Sunday Robotics, a California-based startup, tells me he worries that companies like his don’t stand much of a chance against Chinese firms, which can bring on more workers, like BAAI’s teleoperators, to train robot models and rapidly roll out new hardware. “The iteration speed, the US is losing there,” he says. “And honestly I don’t know how we can win.”
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，加州初创公司 Sunday Robotics 的联合创始人兼首席执行官赵忠（Tony Zhao）告诉我，他担心像他这样的公司几乎没有机会与中国企业竞争。这些企业可以招募更多工人，例如 BA AI 的远程操作员，来训练机器人模型并快速推出新硬件。“在迭代速度上，美国在这方面正在落后，”他说。“说实话，我不知道我们如何才能赢。”

paragraphs.en: To try to keep up, Zhao recently recruited an executive from a Chinese robotics company with deep connections and experience tapping into China’s vast and complex supply chain. “The only way we can beat Chinese companies is to build a China team,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: 为了追赶，赵最近招募了一位来自中国机器人工程公司的高管，该公司与中国庞大且复杂的供应链有着深厚的联系和经验。“我们唯一能赢过中国公司的方式就是组建一个中国团队，”他表示。

paragraphs.en: Some US CEOs, including Demis Hassabis of Google DeepMind and Lachy Groom of Physical Intelligence, both of whom are chasing the robo-ChatGPT moment, have told me they imagine robotics development roughly mirroring that of smartphones, where China makes the hardware and the US makes the brains. (Except that Huawei is now making both.)
paragraphs.zh: 一些美国 CEO，包括 Google DeepMind 的 Demis Hassabis 和 Physical Intelligence 的 Lachy Groom——这两位都在追逐“机器人版 ChatGPT 时刻”——告诉我，他们想象中的机器人发展路径大致会像智能手机一样：由中国制造硬件，美国提供大脑。（当然，华为现在两者都在做。）

paragraphs.en: The answer might be that the US government needs to get involved, suggests Jonathan Hurst, cofounder and chief robot officer of Agility, which makes humanoids. He’s imagining, among other things, heavy investment in advanced domestic manufacturing, such as tax incentives for firms that use robots in their warehouses and factories, as a way to prop up domestic robotics firms. Such a strategy might start to mimic the Chinese government’s patient capital investment in its industries. “We have to be very smart about automation,” he says. “It is the only way.”
paragraphs.zh: 雅各布·赫斯特（Jonathan Hurst）——Agility 的联合创始人兼首席机器人官，这家公司制造类人机器人——认为，也许美国政府需要介入。他设想在其他方面，包括大规模投资先进的本土制造业，比如对在仓库和工厂中使用机器人的企业给予税收激励，以扶持美国国内机器人企业。这样的策略可能开始向中国政府对本国产业的耐心资本投入靠拢。他说：“我们必须对自动化非常聪明，这样做是唯一的出路。”

paragraphs.en: My hotel in Beijing, in the high-tech hub of Zhongguancun, didn’t have any of the wheeled robots that routinely deliver items to guests’ rooms at some big-city hotels. Instead, mine had an unfailingly polite human called Stephen. When I needed to get a shirt cleaned, Stephen got the job done in just a few hours. As I flew home at the end of my trip, I reflected on how many hands had washed, pressed, packaged, and transported the garment back at such speed. Even in China, the robots have not yet won.
paragraphs.zh: 我在北京的酒店坐落于中关村这座高科技中心，并没有看到那些在一些大城市酒店里会定期为客人送餐送物到客房的轮式机器人。取而代之的是一个总是彬彬有礼的人，叫 Stephen。当我需要把一件衬衫送去洗熨时，Stephen 只花了几个小时就把活儿处理好了。行程结束飞回美国前，我不由得想到这件衣服从拿到、清洗、熨烫、打包到运回，经过了多少双手的协作，速度竟如此之快。即便在中国，机器人也还没有赢出来。

paragraphs.zh: 你怎么看？请在下方评论区告诉我们您对这篇文章的看法。或者，您也可以写信给编辑部，邮箱是 [email protected]。
